news.headline: Médecins Sans Frontières Launches 'Keep Life Breathing' Roving Exhibition to Spotlight Humanitarian Crises and Ebola Disease Outbreak in DRC
news.body: HONG KONG, July 6, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) Hong Kong yesterday held an opening ceremony for its 'Keep Life Breathing' Roving Exhibition. As a key part of its annual 'MSF Day' campaign, the exhibition will tour three locations throughout July: The Wai, Festival Walk, and TKO Plaza. It showcases real-life stories of MSF providing emergency medical humanitarian assistance to people affected by protracted conflicts and crises. The event also features an Ebola disease-themed zone, which aims to deepen public awareness and understanding of the ongoing outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC).

logs.summary:
The Ebola outbreak in the DRC has escalated, with confirmed cases surpassing 2,000 and 754 deaths, while the WHO reports the true scale may be 2 to 4 times larger. A U.S. aid worker, the second American infected in this outbreak, has tested positive for Ebola, and international health agencies are scrambling to contain the spread.

New reports indicate that the majority of Ebola cases in eastern Congo are from unknown chains of transmission, complicating containment efforts. Additionally, dozens of workers at an Ebola treatment center in northeast Congo have gone on strike over unpaid salaries, further straining the response. The case and death counts (2,011 cases, 754 deaths) remain consistent with prior reports.

The measles outbreak in Bangladesh continues with renewed severity: between July 13 and 15, at least seven more children died from measles or suspected measles symptoms, and over 900 new suspected cases were reported in a single 24-hour period. The total confirmed cases since March 15 have reached 13,907, and the death toll has risen to 771. Health authorities are now facing an additional challenge as a major dengue fever outbreak simultaneously threatens to overwhelm hospitals.

The measles outbreak in Bangladesh continues to worsen, with eight more children dying from measles-like symptoms in the past 24 hours, raising the death toll to 779 (684 suspected, 95 laboratory-confirmed). Additionally, 974 new suspected cases were recorded, and total confirmed cases since March 15 have reached 14,104.

logs.summary:
As of July 14, China's National Meteorological Center issued an orange alert for rainstorms, noting that 169 tailings ponds are affected by rainfall. Typhoon Bavi, the strongest storm to hit mainland China this year, forced over 260,000 people to evacuate their homes in Liaoning province. Additionally, videos surfaced of hundreds of snakes escaping into floodwaters, reportedly after Typhoon Maysak.

Record rainfall in China led to residents paddleboarding and swimming through flooded streets. Floodwaters swept away vehicles in Chengde following Typhoon Bavi, while emergency workers reinforced dikes in Shenyang. Torrential rains and widespread flooding continued to disrupt life across eastern China.

logs.summary:
Venezuela's Acting President Delcy Rodríguez publicly thanked Caricom countries for their aid as the death toll from the twin earthquakes remained at 4,829, with thousands homeless and families continuing to search rubble. The earthquake recovery effort has also overshadowed the country's push for democracy, according to analysis.

New reports detail ongoing recovery: the death toll remains at 4,490, while NASA radar data reveals significant ground shifts near La Guaira. Aid continues from Florida, Guyana, and Houston, bolstered by U.S. State Department support, and volunteers search tirelessly for missing victims. Lawmakers have urged lifting sanctions, and Venezuela’s inflation spike is compounding earthquake misery.

New reports as of July 14 show the earthquake death toll has risen to between 4,561 and 4,734. A Palm Beach County Fire Rescue team successfully rescued a man buried in rubble, and aid continues to flow from Florida and other regions to support recovery efforts.

logs.summary:
The death toll from the twin earthquakes has officially risen to 4,829, according to government figures. The European Union has pledged an additional €20 million in humanitarian aid, while US troops unloaded China's only known aid flight at the scene. Reports also highlight engineers assessing structural damage, a benefit concert featuring Marc Anthony and Ricardo Montaner, and ongoing grassroots fundraising efforts from Florida to Minnesota.

The death toll from the twin earthquakes was officially confirmed at 4,829 by National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez. A fake image of a dog protecting a child went viral and was debunked by fact-checkers. Meanwhile, recovery efforts include a World Cup halftime tribute featuring the NY Philharmonic and Gustavo Dudamel, and an opinion piece highlights the prioritization of critical health services in the aftermath.

logs.summary:
A wildfire erupted in the historic Fontainebleau forest south of Paris, burning over 2,000 hectares and prompting the evacuation of about 1,000 residents. French authorities arrested two people suspected of deliberately starting the blaze, while hundreds of firefighters and waterbombing aircraft worked to contain the fire as Europe faced another heatwave.

The wildfires in the Fontainebleau forest have shown mixed status: some reports indicate containment, while others report the fire is still spreading with 850 firefighters and 200 vehicles deployed. Authorities discovered ten ignition points and arrested two suspects on suspicion of arson.

On July 15, approximately 800 firefighters continued to tackle flare-ups in the Fontainebleau forest as French President Emmanuel Macron pledged to replant the historic forest. Meanwhile, authorities are investigating a volunteer firefighter as a suspect in starting the devastating blaze, which burned over 2,000 hectares near Paris.

incidents.summary:
A magnitude 5.9 earthquake struck 42 km north-northwest of Te Anau, New Zealand, on July 16, 2026, initially reported as a 6.3 magnitude. The quake shook buildings across the South Island's tourist region and prompted a brief tsunami warning, which was later downgraded to a marine advisory. No injuries or damage were reported, and authorities lifted the tsunami alert shortly after.

A tsunami warning and immediate evacuation order were issued for parts of the South Island's west coast following the earthquake, but the alert has since been lifted. The mayor of the affected area reported property damage, and GeoNet recorded several lighter aftershocks after the main quake.

incidents.canonical_name: Earthquake 90 km SW of Puerto Madero, Mexico 2026-07
incidents.summary:
A powerful magnitude 7.3–7.4 earthquake struck off the coast of Mexico's southern Chiapas state on July 17, 2026, prompting a tsunami warning for nearby coasts of Mexico and Guatemala, with waves up to one meter possible. People evacuated buildings in affected areas, while authorities confirmed no tsunami threat to Hawaii.
A powerful earthquake of magnitude 7.3–7.4 struck off the coast of Chiapas, Mexico, near Puerto Madero on July 17, 2026, at a shallow depth of about 10 km. The quake triggered a tsunami warning and was strongly felt in Guatemala and El Salvador, prompting evacuations in some areas. No immediate damage or fatalities were reported, though a series of aftershocks were recorded.
New reports indicate that the 7.3-magnitude earthquake off the coast of Chiapas, Mexico, caused two injuries and cracked some buildings, though no major damage or fatalities were reported.

No immediate damage or fatalities were reported, though a series of aftershocks were recorded.
New reports indicate that the 7.3-magnitude earthquake off the coast of Chiapas, Mexico, caused two injuries and cracked some buildings, though no major damage or fatalities were reported.

incidents.canonical_name: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda
incidents.summary: The Ebola outbreak in DR Congo has surged by about 70% over the past two weeks, with over 40 new cases reported daily. Confirmed cases have risen to 2,181 with 864 deaths, and most deaths are occurring outside health facilities due to funeral practices. A US citizen working for a humanitarian organization has tested positive, and US aid workers have been quarantined in Kenya as the CDC expands travel restrictions.
